title: ageratina choricephala
intro: Ageratina choricephala, also known as Mexican Tea or Hierba de Yerba, is a perennial plant native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Ageratina choricephala include its potential to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Ageratina choricephala can be prepared as an herbal tea by infusing the leaves in hot water, or used in tinctures, capsules, and other herbal preparations to harness its medicinal properties.
